% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(Nr) :- Nr = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(Nc) :- Nc = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output dimensions: one row less than the input and the same number of columns
out_rows(OR) :- OR = Nr - 1.
out_cols(Oc) :- Oc = Nc.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Identify the two nonzero cells (they are assumed to be exactly two)
input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.
input(BR2, BC2, V2), V2 != 0,
BR1 != BR2, BC1 != BC2,
BR1 + BC1 <= BR2 + BC2.

% Offsets (the sum of row and columnindices) and their difference
offset1 = BR1 + BC1.
offset2 = BR2 + BC2.
diff    = offset2 - offset1.               % diff  0 because of the ordering above
Dtemp   = diff * 2.                         % Dtemp = 2diff

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Choose the period D.
% If offset2 is even we use D = Dtemp2, otherwise D = Dtemp.
offset2_even(Kpar) :- offset2 = 2 * Kpar.   % Kpar is an integer, so offset2 is even
% Two mutually exclusive choices for D
1 { D = Dtemp }           1 :- not offset2_even(0).
1 { D = Dtemp - 2 }       1 :- offset2_even(0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Orientation: columnoriented if the first nonzero cell is not in column0,
% otherwise roworiented.
orientation_is_column :- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0, BC != 0.
orientation_is_row    :- not orientation_is_column.   % all other nonzeros must be in column0

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Position conditions for the two values.
% Columnoriented case (use column % D)
output_cell(R_out, C_out, V1) :- orientation_is_column,
    C_out = D * K + offset1, 
    K >= 0, K < 1000, C_out < out_cols(Oc),
    input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.
output_cell(R_out, C_out, V2) :- orientation_is_column,
    C_out = D * K + offset2, 
    K >= 0, K < 1000, C_out < out_cols(Oc),
    input(BR2, BC2, V2), V2 != 0.
output_cell(R_out, C_out, 0) :- orientation_is_column,
    not (C_out = D * K + offset1) or not (C_out = D * K + offset2),
    orientation_is_column,
    input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.

% Roworiented case (use row % D)
output_cell(R_out, C_out, V1) :- not orientation_is_column,
    R_out = D * K + offset1,
    K >= 0, K < 1000, R_out < out_rows(OR),
    input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.
output_cell(R_out, C_out, V2) :- not orientation_is_column,
    R_out = D * K + offset2,
    K >= 0, K < 1000, R_out < out_rows(OR),
    input(BR2, BC2, V2), V2 != 0.
output_cell(R_out, C_out, 0) :- not orientation_is_column,
    not (R_out = D * K + offset1) and not (R_out = D * K + offset2),
    not orientation_is_column,
    input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. The final output predicate.
output(R_out, C_out, Color) :- output_cell(R_out, C_out, Color).

% Exactly one answer per output cell.
1 { output(R_out, C_out, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- output_cell(R_out, C_out).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 1. Dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(Nr) :- Nr = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(Nc) :- Nc = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output dimensions: one row less than the input and the same number of columns
out_rows(OR) :- OR = Nr - 1.
out_cols(Oc) :- Oc = Nc.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 2. Identify the two nonzero cells (they are assumed to be exactly two)
input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.
input(BR2, BC2, V2), V2 != 0,
BR1 != BR2, BC1 != BC2,
BR1 + BC1 <= BR2 + BC2.

% Offsets (the sum of row and columnindices) and their difference
offset1 = BR1 + BC1.
offset2 = BR2 + BC2.
diff    = offset2 - offset1.               % diff  0 because of the ordering above
Dtemp   = diff * 2.                         % Dtemp = 2diff

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 3. Choose the period D.
% If offset2 is even we use D = Dtemp2, otherwise D = Dtemp.
offset2_even(Kpar) :- offset2 = 2 * Kpar.   % Kpar is an integer, so offset2 is even
% Two mutually exclusive choices for D
1 { D = Dtemp }           1 :- not offset2_even(0).
1 { D = Dtemp - 2 }       1 :- offset2_even(0).

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 4. Orientation: columnoriented if the first nonzero cell is not in column0,
% otherwise roworiented.
orientation_is_column :- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0, BC != 0.
orientation_is_row    :- not orientation_is_column.   % all other nonzeros must be in column0

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 5. Position conditions for the two values.
% Columnoriented case (use column % D)
output_cell(R_out, C_out, V1) :- orientation_is_column,
    C_out = D * K + offset1, 
    K >= 0, K < 1000, C_out < out_cols(Oc),
    input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.
output_cell(R_out, C_out, V2) :- orientation_is_column,
    C_out = D * K + offset2, 
    K >= 0, K < 1000, C_out < out_cols(Oc),
    input(BR2, BC2, V2), V2 != 0.
output_cell(R_out, C_out, 0) :- orientation_is_column,
    not (C_out = D * K + offset1) or not (C_out = D * K + offset2),
    orientation_is_column,
    input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.

% Roworiented case (use row % D)
output_cell(R_out, C_out, V1) :- not orientation_is_column,
    R_out = D * K + offset1,
    K >= 0, K < 1000, R_out < out_rows(OR),
    input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.
output_cell(R_out, C_out, V2) :- not orientation_is_column,
    R_out = D * K + offset2,
    K >= 0, K < 1000, R_out < out_rows(OR),
    input(BR2, BC2, V2), V2 != 0.
output_cell(R_out, C_out, 0) :- not orientation_is_column,
    not (R_out = D * K + offset1) and not (R_out = D * K + offset2),
    not orientation_is_column,
    input(BR1, BC1, V1), V1 != 0.

% ----------------------------------------------------------------------
% 6. The final output predicate.
output(R_out, C_out, Color) :- output_cell(R_out, C_out, Color).

% Exactly one answer per output cell.
1 { output(R_out, C_out, Color) : color(C) } 1 :- output_cell(R_out, C_out).
